pattern: A recursive-descent or stack evaluation of a nested boolean grammar with NOT, AND, and OR operators applied to comma-separated subexpressions.
hints: Each operator is followed by a parenthesized list of subexpressions; the work is grouping those subexpressions correctly.
A stack lets you push values and operators, and on a closing parenthesis you fold everything since the matching open paren.

Scan the string: push 't'/'f' as booleans and operators as markers; on ')' pop booleans until the operator, combine them per the operator (NOT inverts the single value, AND/OR reduce the list), and push the result.

edge_inputs.description: nested OR inside AND from the first example
NOT of a single true is false

stress.description: randomly generated valid boolean expressions of bounded depth
